words.def: a long, narrow opening, into which you put or fit something
words.example: to put some coins in the slot

words.def: to break something, or to be broken, violently and noisily into many pieces
words.example: Several windows had been smashed.

words.def: to break something suddenly with a sharp noise; to be broken in this way
words.example: snap something, The wind had snapped the tree in two.

words.def: to put something in liquid for a time so that it becomes completely wet; to become completely wet in this way
words.example: soak something (in something), I usually soak the beans overnight.

words.def: if the value, amount or level of something soars, it rises very quickly
words.example: soaring costs/prices/temperatures

words.def: connected with socialism
words.example: socialist beliefs

words.def: only; single
words.example: the sole surviving member of the family

words.def: only; not involving somebody/something else
words.example: She was motivated solely by self-interest.

words.def: a lawyer who prepares legal documents, for example for the sale of land or buildings, advises people on legal matters, and can speak for them in some courts of law
words.example: Her first step was to contact a solicitor for advice.

words.def: support by one person or group of people for another because they share feelings, opinions, aims, etc.
words.example: community solidarity

words.def: done by one person alone, without anyone helping them
words.example: his first solo flight

words.def: sensible; that you can rely on and that will probably give good results
words.example: He's a person of very sound judgement.

words.def: complete power to govern a country
words.example: The country claimed sovereignty over the island.

words.def: advertising material sent by email to people who have not asked for it; advertising material on the internet that is not wanted
words.example: to send/block spam

words.def: the length of time that something lasts or is able to continue
words.example: I worked with him over a span of six years.

words.def: available to do what you want with rather than work
words.example: He's studying music in his spare time.

words.def: to cause something to start or develop, especially suddenly
words.example: spark something, The proposal would spark a storm of protest around the country.

words.def: designed or developed for a particular purpose or area of knowledge
words.example: specialized equipment

words.def: a detailed description of how something is, or should be, designed or made
words.example: the technical specifications of the new model (= of car)

words.def: a small amount of something that shows what the rest of it is like
words.example: Astronauts have brought back specimens of rock from the moon.

words.def: two lenses in a frame that rests on the nose and ears. People wear spectacles in order to be able to see better or to protect their eyes from bright light.
words.example: a pair of spectacles

words.def: a band of coloured lights in order of their wavelengths, as seen in a rainbow and into which light may be separated
words.example: A spectrum is formed by a ray of light passing through a prism.

words.def: a short period of time during which something lasts
words.example: We had a spell of warm weather in April.

words.def: a solid figure that is completely round, with every point on its surface at an equal distance from the centre
words.example: The Earth is not a perfect sphere.

words.def: a very fast turning movement
words.example: Only in the last 50 years have we had clocks accurate enough to measure changes in the earth's spin.

words.def: the row of small bones that are connected together down the middle of the back
words.example: A shiver went down my spine.

words.def: a light with a single, very bright beam that can be directed at a particular place or person, especially a performer on the stage
words.example: The room was lit by spotlights.

words.def: a husband or wife
words.example: Fill in your spouse’s name here.

words.def: a person who tries to get secret information about another country, organization or person, especially somebody who is employed by a government or the police
words.example: He was denounced as a foreign spy.

words.def: a section of a police force that deals with a particular type of crime
words.example: the drugs/fraud/bomb/riot squad

words.def: to press something, especially with your fingers
words.example: to squeeze a tube of toothpaste

words.def: to push a sharp, pointed object, especially a knife, into somebody, killing or injuring them
words.example: He was stabbed to death in a racist attack.

words.def: the quality or state of being steady and not changing or being upset in any way (= the quality of being stable)
words.example: political/economic/social stability

words.def: to become or to make something become steady and unlikely to change; to make something stable
words.example: The patient's condition stabilized.

words.def: money that somebody invests in a company
words.example: a 20 per cent stake in the business

words.def: done from a position in which you are standing rather than sitting or running
words.example: a standing jump/start

words.def: unpleasant; real, and impossible to avoid
words.example: The author paints a stark picture of life in a prison camp.

words.def: connected with or based on statistics
words.example: statistical analysis

words.def: to control the direction in which a boat, car, etc. moves
words.example: He steered the boat into the harbour.

words.def: the main long, thin part of a plant above the ground from which the leaves or flowers grow; a smaller part that grows from this and supports flowers or leaves
words.example: long, trailing stems of ivy

words.def: a fixed idea or image that many people have of a particular type of person or thing, but which is often not true in reality and may cause hurt and offence
words.example: cultural/gender/racial stereotypes

words.def: something that helps somebody/something to develop better or more quickly
words.example: stimulus for something, Books provide children with ideas and a stimulus for play.

words.def: to move a liquid or substance around, using a spoon or something similar, in order to mix it completely
words.example: stir something, She stirred her tea.

words.def: the process of keeping something in a particular place until it is needed; the space where things can be kept
words.example: tables that fold flat for storage

words.def: easy to do or to understand; not complicated
words.example: It's a relatively straightforward process.

words.def: pressure on a system or relationship because great demands are being placed on it
words.example: The transport service cannot cope with the strain of so many additional passengers.

words.def: a single thin piece of thread, wire, hair, etc.
words.example: He pulled at a loose strand of wool in his sweater.

words.def: done as part of a plan that is meant to achieve a particular purpose or to gain an advantage
words.example: strategic planning

words.def: interesting and unusual enough to attract attention
words.example: a striking feature

words.def: a long narrow piece of paper, metal, cloth, etc.
words.example: a strip of material

words.def: to try very hard to achieve something
words.example: strive for something, We encourage all members to strive for the highest standards.

words.def: connected with the way in which something is built or organized
words.example: Storms have caused structural damage to hundreds of homes.

words.def: to hit your foot against something while you are walking or running and almost fall
words.example: The child stumbled and fell.

words.def: to make a person or an animal unconscious for a short time, especially by hitting them on the head
words.example: The fall stunned me for a moment.

words.def: the act of accepting that somebody has defeated you and that you must obey them
words.example: a gesture of submission

words.def: a person who pays money, usually once a year, to receive regular copies of a magazine or newspaper or have access to it online
words.example: subscribers to ‘New Scientist’

words.def: an amount of money that you pay regularly to receive a service, be a member of a club, support a charity or receive regular copies of a newspaper or magazine; the act of paying this money
words.example: an annual subscription

words.def: money that is paid by a government or an organization to reduce the costs of services or of producing goods so that their prices can be kept low
words.example: agricultural subsidies

words.def: large in amount, value or importance
words.example: substantial sums of money

words.def: very much; a lot
words.example: The costs have increased substantially.

words.def: a person or thing that you use or have instead of the one you normally use or have
words.example: a meat substitute

words.def: an act of using one person or thing in the place of another
words.example: Two substitutions were made during the game.

words.def: not very obvious or easy to notice
words.example: subtle colours/flavours/smells, etc.

words.def: in or connected with a suburb
words.example: suburban areas

words.def: a number of people or things that follow each other in time or order
words.example: a succession of visitors

words.def: following immediately one after the other
words.example: This was their fourth successive win.

words.def: a person or thing that comes after somebody/something else and takes their/its place
words.example: Who's the likely successor to him as party leader?

words.def: to take liquid, air, etc. into your mouth by using the muscles of your lips
words.example: to suck the juice from an orange

words.def: to make a claim against a person or an organization in court about something that they have said or done to harm you
words.example: They threatened to sue if the work was not completed.

words.def: the act of killing yourself deliberately
words.example: to commit suicide

words.def: a set of rooms, especially in a hotel
words.example: a hotel/private/honeymoon suite

words.def: the highest point of something, especially the top of a mountain
words.example: We reached the summit at noon.

words.def: excellent; of very good quality
words.example: a superb player

words.def: better in quality than somebody/something else; greater than somebody/something else
words.example: vastly superior

words.def: to be in charge of somebody/something and make sure that everything is done correctly, safely, etc.
words.example: supervise (somebody/something), to supervise building work

words.def: the work or activity involved in being in charge of somebody/something and making sure that everything is done correctly, safely, etc.
words.example: Very young children should not be left to play without supervision.

words.def: a person who supervises somebody/something
words.example: I have a meeting with my supervisor about my research topic.

words.def: a thing that is added to something else to improve or complete it
words.example: vitamin/dietary supplements (= vitamins and other foods eaten in addition to what you usually eat)

words.def: encouraging somebody or giving them help or sympathy
words.example: a supportive family

words.def: according to what is generally thought or believed but not known for certain
words.example: The novel is supposedly based on a true story.

words.def: to put an end, often by force, to a group or an activity that is believed to threaten authority
words.example: The rebellion was brutally suppressed.

words.def: highest in rank or position
words.example: the Supreme Commander of the armed forces

words.def: a sudden increase of a strong feeling
words.example: She felt a sudden surge of anger.

words.def: used in or connected with surgery
words.example: surgical procedures

words.def: an amount that is extra or more than you need
words.example: food surpluses

words.def: to admit that you have been defeated and want to stop fighting; to allow yourself to be caught, taken prisoner, etc.
words.example: The rebel soldiers were forced to surrender.

words.def: the act of carefully watching a person suspected of a crime or a place where a crime may be committed
words.example: The police are keeping the suspects under constant surveillance.

words.def: the act of officially removing somebody from their job, school, team, etc. for a period of time, usually as a punishment
words.example: suspension from school

words.def: a feeling that somebody has done something wrong, illegal or dishonest, even though you have no proof
words.example: They drove away slowly to avoid arousing suspicion.

words.def: feeling that somebody has done something wrong, illegal or dishonest, without having any proof
words.example: They became suspicious of his behaviour and contacted the police.

words.def: to provide enough of what somebody/something needs in order to live or exist
words.example: Which planets can sustain life?

words.def: a swinging movement or rhythm
words.example: He took a wild swing at the ball.

words.def: a weapon with a long metal blade (= sharp cutting part) and a handle
words.example: to draw/sheathe a sword (= to take it out of/put it into its cover)

words.def: containing symbols, or being used as a symbol
words.example: He shook his fist in a symbolic gesture of defiance.

words.def: a set of physical conditions that show you have a particular disease or medical problem
words.example: PMS or premenstrual syndrome

words.def: the act of combining separate ideas, beliefs, styles, etc.; a mixture or combination of ideas, beliefs, styles, etc.
words.example: synthesis of A with B, the synthesis of art with everyday life

words.def: done according to a system or plan, in a complete, efficient or determined way
words.example: a systematic approach to solving the problem

words.def: an act of trying to take the ball from an opponent in football (soccer), hockey, etc.; an act of knocking somebody to the ground, for example in rugby or American football
words.example: He was booked for a late tackle on Torres.

words.def: the particular method you use to achieve something
words.example: They tried all kinds of tactics to get us to go.

words.def: connected with the particular method you use to achieve something
words.example: tactical planning

words.def: a person who pays tax to the government, especially on the money that they earn
words.example: Hundreds of thousands of pounds of taxpayers' money (= money paid in taxes) have been spent on the project.

words.def: to attract somebody or make somebody want to do or have something, even if they know it is wrong
words.example: tempt somebody, I was tempted by the dessert menu.

words.def: a person who pays rent for the use of a room, building, land, etc. to the person who owns it
words.example: They had evicted their tenants for non-payment of rent.

words.def: kind, gentle and loving
words.example: tender words

words.def: the period of time when somebody holds an important job, especially a political one; the act of holding an important job
words.example: his four-year tenure as president

words.def: that cannot be cured and will lead to death, often slowly
words.example: He has terminal lung cancer.

words.def: to end; to make something end
words.example: Your contract of employment terminates in December.

words.def: used to refer to an area of land when you are mentioning its natural features, for example, if it is rough, flat, etc.
words.example: difficult/rough/mountainous terrain

words.def: excellent; wonderful
words.example: I feel absolutely terrific today!

words.def: to make a statement that something happened or that something is true, especially as a witness in court
words.example: testify against somebody/something, She refused to testify against her husband.

words.def: a thing that shows that something else exists or is true
words.example: This increase in exports bears testimony to the successes of industry.

words.def: the way a surface, substance or piece of cloth feels when you touch it, for example how rough, smooth, hard or soft it is
words.example: the soft texture of velvet

words.def: used to show that you are pleased that something good has happened or that something bad has been avoided
words.example: There was a fire in the building, but thankfully no one was hurt.

words.def: connected with the theatre
words.example: a theatrical agent

words.def: the study of religion and beliefs
words.example: a degree in theology

words.def: connected with the ideas and principles on which a particular subject is based, rather than with practice and experiment
words.example: a theoretical approach

words.def: after the time or event mentioned
words.example: She married at 17 and gave birth to her first child shortly thereafter.

words.def: used to introduce the result of the action or situation mentioned
words.example: Regular exercise strengthens the heart, thereby reducing the risk of heart attack.

words.def: making people think seriously about a particular subject or issue
words.example: a brilliant and thought-provoking play

words.def: quiet, because you are thinking
words.example: He looked thoughtful.
